title: This former hacker saw the light—and now wants to collect all of it
gist: Profile of Dan Roelker (ex-SpaceX/DARPA/OpenSea) and his company Observable Space, now the largest US-based telescope manufacturer, betting that ground-based optics for space-domain awareness and laser communications — driven by satellite proliferation and orbital data centers — is the next space race.

insights.claim: Observable Space, formed ~18 months ago by merging OurSky with telescope-maker PlaneWave Instruments, has become the largest US-based telescope manufacturer, offering software, adaptive optics, and hardware for space-domain awareness.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: A consolidating domestic optics supplier reshoring telescope manufacturing from China; positioned at the center of space-tracking and lasercom demand.

insights.claim: The US Space Force awarded Observable Space a $94 million contract last month to expand production of high-performance, mobile off-grid robotic telescopes for space domain awareness.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Concrete defense revenue and demand signal for ground-based space-tracking infrastructure.
insights.quote: The Department is acting on the urgent need for mobile, off-grid robotic telescopes

insights.claim: Observable Space won the contract to build all 1,200 telescopes (11-inch mirrors each, mimicking an 8-meter optical telescope) for the Eric Schmidt-funded Argus Array imaging the entire Northern Hemisphere sky.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Large philanthropic-backed order demonstrates scaling demand for arrays of small telescopes over single large ones.

insights.claim: Observable Space closed a $90 million Series A in late May 2026 to largely accelerate its laser communications business.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Capital is flowing into ground-based lasercom infrastructure; a fundable thesis around space-to-ground optical links.

insights.claim: Active satellites in low-Earth orbit grew from about 3,000 to more than 15,000 over the past half-decade, driven by Starlink, Amazon Leo, and China's Guowang and Qianfan constellations, forcing the US military to rely more on commercial tracking data.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Satellite proliferation is the structural demand driver for ground-based tracking; commercial space-domain-awareness providers benefit.
insights.locator: The ways in which the US military tracks satellites

insights.claim: Roelker frames the next space race as ground-based: control of light (collecting the most of it) determines control of space.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: A contrarian framing that the high-value layer is ground optics, not launch — guides where Observable Space invests vs competing with SpaceX.
insights.quote: The new space race is going to be on the ground

themes.theme: Laser communications and orbital data centers

insights.claim: Optical/laser communications deliver 10 to 100 times the data of radiofrequency from a smaller, lower-power transmitter, but cannot transmit through clouds — requiring many distributed ground stations.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: The cloud limitation creates demand for global networks of optical ground stations, Observable Space's scaling target.

insights.claim: Observable Space is in talks with SpaceX and others on orbital data centers, which need high-bandwidth space-to-ground links, and positions itself to build the in-space relay systems that become the 'fiber optics infrastructure' of Solar System communication.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Orbital data centers are an emerging high-bandwidth-link market; lasercom ground infrastructure is a pick-and-shovel play.
insights.quote: We're going to be building the systems that get deployed in space and become the fiber optics infrastructure of communication across the Solar System

insights.claim: Observable Space facilitated optical communications on Artemis II in April 2026 via an RC700 system at Mount Stromlo, with high-bandwidth lasercom expected to become standard for future Artemis missions enabling HD lunar landings.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Flight heritage on a flagship NASA program de-risks Observable Space's lasercom offering for future contracts.
insights.locator: But it is starting to happen

insights.claim: Observable Space's Iguana space telescope (200mm aperture) for on-orbit space domain awareness and rendezvous navigation will first fly later this year on an Apex bus as part of Project Shadow demonstrating space-based interceptors.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Expansion into on-orbit optics and defense interceptor demos broadens Observable Space's addressable market.

insights.claim: Most telescope manufacturing, including big players like Celestron, had moved offshore to China, leaving PlaneWave Instruments (founded by an ex-Celestron engineer) as a domestic Michigan-based maker that became the core of Observable Space's hardware.
insights.speaker: Eric Berger
insights.so_what: Domestic optics supply is strategically scarce; reshored manufacturing is a national-security and supply-chain advantage.
insights.locator: Neither had much experience with telescopes
